First, the simplest response to the question of the reason for Jesus's attention to the children is that he loved them and desired to heal them. Perhaps Jesus's loving reciprocation of the longing that he felt from his hearers was to turn and bless those most vulnerable—the sick and afflicted, and the children. Those innocents had lived through the trauma of the cataclysmic events marking Christ's crucifixion, terrifying in and of themselves, from which their parents could do little to protect…
